title: PostgreSQL authority and store boundaries defined
objective: Resolve the target PostgreSQL + RuVector macro-state authority against every valid existing redb, CodeDB, AgentDB, external-secret, and projection use case, giving each of the five state families exactly one canonical owner or explicit subordinate role, one synchronization direction, one identity boundary, and one recovery source, with the current CodeDB and envctl redb uses decided under STORE-001 rather than erased by assumption.

work_items.surface: Durable relational + semantic macro-state family (identities, histories, permissions, provenance, module_path metadata, code AST/rows, causal dependency edges, blob references, embeddings, hybrid-search indexes, RuVector GNN edges)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector (single canonical durable owner; RuVector as native No-Sidecar extension)
work_items.role_of_others: redb holds only a transient forward buffer; envctl is the writer bridge; RuVector vector/graph indexes are reproducibly derived from versioned source rows and are never an independent authority
work_items.identity_boundary: SHA-256 content hash for blobs + stable PostgreSQL primary identities; derived-index identity is bound to the source-row identity
work_items.recovery_source: PostgreSQL PITR + reproducible re-embedding of semantic indexes
work_items.no_dual_write: redb never becomes a second durable writer for a PostgreSQL-owned class; no separate vector sidecar
work_items.decision: PostgreSQL + RuVector owns the durable and semantic families exactly once (STORE-001 assignments 1 and 2, closing PGAUTH-001).

work_items.surface: Transient high-frequency redb family (agent thoughts, terminal traces, intermediate logs, state flags, BLAKE3 dedup hashes, application-level WAL/outbox during PostgreSQL latency or outage)
work_items.owner: redb (per edge node / process) as the intended transient tier
work_items.role_of_others: PostgreSQL receives only settled/significant states flushed forward by envctl; the outbox is subordinate to PostgreSQL as destination authority
work_items.sync_direction: redb -> envctl flush -> PostgreSQL (only significant states persist); forward-only
work_items.identity_boundary: agent-local / process-local keys and monotonic outbox sequence + idempotency key (not a shared identity)
work_items.recovery_source: redb local disk (process lifecycle); on loss, transient by definition; outbox replays unacked entries to PostgreSQL
work_items.no_dual_write: redb never becomes durable authority for a PostgreSQL-owned class; the outbox never applies twice (idempotency key)
work_items.decision: redb owns the transient family as its intended terminal role (STORE-001 assignment 3, closing PGAUTH-002's transient rows).

work_items.surface: Per-agent cognition family (MicroLoRA adapters, SONA policy matrices, reinforcement-learning weights, agent learning traces, witness-bound memory)
work_items.owner: AgentDB (.rvf) per agent (canonical only for one agent's cognition)
work_items.role_of_others: non-authoritative over shared macro-state; witnessed by Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256) rooted in PostgreSQL source vectors; shared knowledge promotes one-way into the PostgreSQL knowledge graph
work_items.sync_direction: self-contained; one-way promotion AgentDB -> PostgreSQL; no back-write to per-agent cognition
work_items.identity_boundary: agent identity + .rvf format; SHAKE256 witness chains
work_items.recovery_source: AgentDB .rvf file; regenerable from base model + training history
work_items.no_dual_write: AgentDB cannot silently own shared user-space truth (PGAUTH-003 invariant)
work_items.decision: AgentDB owns the cognitive family as its intended owner of active cognition (STORE-001 assignment 4, closing PGAUTH-003).

work_items.surface: Derived projection family (materialized source directories for musl builds, exported release trees, generated files, build inputs, reports, disposable caches)
work_items.owner: PostgreSQL is the source of truth; envctl / CodeDB materialization is the single deterministic read-only materializer
work_items.role_of_others: filesystem projections are derived and disposable; regenerated deterministically from PostgreSQL rows via module_path metadata
work_items.identity_boundary: canonical source identity + selected branch/version + module_path; SHA-256 byte-exact checksum on materialized output
work_items.no_dual_write: projection output can never become a competing writable owner and never back-writes canonical state (PGAUTH-004 invariants 1 and 2)
work_items.decision: Projections are disposable derivatives with one-way provenance from PostgreSQL (STORE-001 assignment 5, closing PGAUTH-004).

work_items.surface: External source / secret family (API keys, attestation bundles, source pins, externally authoritative services, devices, repositories, credential stores, human-approved records)
work_items.owner: OS keyring + committed source pins (external authority owns); LifeOS holds only a reference
work_items.role_of_others: PostgreSQL stores references and provenance, never raw secret values; redb never holds secrets; external RECORDS may be ingested one-way with a freshness/conflict policy and approval gate
work_items.sync_direction: secret values: n/a (never captured); external records: inbound one-way ingestion with conflict/freshness policy; outbound via governed handoff
work_items.identity_boundary: local reference key (never the secret value)
work_items.recovery_source: keyring + committed pins; fail-closed re-solicit / re-retrieve on outage
work_items.no_dual_write: external service remains authority; LifeOS holds a reference; no unresolved dual-write (PGAUTH-005 invariant 3)
work_items.decision: External authorities own their state; LifeOS references, never captures, secret values (STORE-001 assignment 6, closing PGAUTH-005).

work_items.surface: CodeDB source-blob store currently durable-on-redb (PGREDB-001, codedb_store_redb) — DECIDED, not erased
work_items.owner: Target canonical owner is PostgreSQL via codedb_store_pg::PgStore under the same BlobStore SHA-256 content-addressed identity; redb role after cutover is transient forward buffer
work_items.role_of_others: PgStore is a PEER of the redb store under one BlobStore trait, not a tier above; the current durable-on-redb default is TRANSITIONAL
work_items.sync_direction: capture -> store (selectable backend); forward-only; a class leaves redb-durable only after a green live blobstore_parity.rs run against a disposable PostgreSQL
work_items.identity_boundary: SHA-256 content hash of file bytes (identical across backends)
work_items.recovery_source: redb file + working tree today; PostgreSQL PITR after the verified cutover
work_items.no_dual_write: one SHA-256 identity across backends; the class migrates one verified swap at a time, never by assumption
work_items.decision: The durable-today CodeDB redb blob store is decided as a transitional implementation that migrates to PostgreSQL-canonical under the existing SHA-256 parity contract, honoring upgrades-not-downgrades via the migration MECHANISM (STORE-001 correction; ADOPT-001 BlobStore/PgStore/SHA-256).

work_items.surface: envctl migration/orchestration state currently durable-on-redb (PGREDB-002, migration_db: targets, runs, run_events, approvals, checkpoints, rollbacks) — DECIDED, not erased
work_items.owner: envctl redb is the transient runtime store for in-flight migration orchestration; settled/significant audit history is promoted one-way to PostgreSQL DURABLE-RELATIONAL audit tables under the same verified-cutover discipline
work_items.role_of_others: envctl is NOT the synchronizing durable writer of macro-history; PostgreSQL owns the promoted audit history (AUTH-CLAIM-001 refuted)
work_items.sync_direction: envctl redb runtime -> one-way promote settled audit -> PostgreSQL
work_items.recovery_source: redb file + envctl replayable migration engine (runtime); PostgreSQL PITR for promoted audit history
work_items.no_dual_write: no undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write; the migration audit class cuts over per-class only after verified parity
work_items.decision: The durable-today envctl redb migration store is decided as a transient runtime store whose settled audit history promotes forward to PostgreSQL, preserved rather than erased (inventory PGREDB-002, REDB-CLAIM-020 qualified; ADOPT-002 envctl; STORE-001 transient class + verified-cutover invariant).

completion_gate: Every durable, transient, cognitive, projected, and external state family has exactly one canonical owner or explicit subordinate role, synchronization direction, identity boundary, recovery source, and no unresolved dual-write authority; current CodeDB and envctl redb uses are decided rather than erased by assumption.
downstream: PGAUTH-006 reconciles these five family boundaries with the seven concrete redb usage rows into one closed synchronization-and-recovery matrix; POSTGRES-002 (whole user-space relational graph) remains Draft until the global graph gate is closed.
